title: 7 AI-powered scams Indians are falling for in 2026: Deepfakes, fake HR calls and more
summary: AI-powered scams are becoming more sophisticated in India, with fraudsters using cloned voices, deepfake videos and realistic phishing emails to target users. From fake HR interviews and “digital arrest” scams to fraudulent AI trading apps, cybercriminals are exploiting trust at scale.

summary: OpenAI is establishing its first applied AI lab outside the United States in Singapore. The company plans to hire around 200 people in the coming years. title: Google DeepMind hires staff from Contextual AI in licensing deal, source says
summary: Google DeepMind is reportedly acquiring over 20 researchers and licensing technology from AI startup Contextual AI for $80 million to $90 million. This move follows Alphabet's strategy of talent acquisition through licensing deals, similar to past agreements with Windsurf and Character.AI.

title: Meta offers AI rival chatbots limited free WhatsApp access, sources say
summary: Meta Platforms has offered European AI chatbots free access to its WhatsApp messaging service, with charges to be implemented after a usage limit is reached. This proposal aims to appease EU regulators investigating Meta's market practices.

title: Meta begins 8,000 global job cuts in Asian hub of Singapore
summary: Meta Platforms is laying off approximately 8,000 employees globally as part of a restructuring to boost efficiency and reduce costs, while simultaneously investing heavily in artificial intelligence. title: Govt’s new ‘law-to-code’ push; UpGrad’s $1.7 billion valuation
summary: Happy Wednesday! The government plans to translate legal provisions into software code that enforces compliance by design. This and more in today's ETtech Morning Dispatch.

title: Coded compliance: Centre is eyeing 'law-to-code' to AI-proof data law
summary: Law-to-code is the practice of translating legal rules into machine-executable algorithms such as a Python code that software can automatically process and enforce without human intervention.

title: Sacked H-1B staff asked to do more B-2 paperwork
summary: Tech giants such as Amazon, Oracle, and Meta have laid off thousands of employees in the past few months, and a large number of the affected people are estimated to be from India on H-1B visas. The visa rules give them 60 days to find new employment, or they must return to their home countries.

title: Speed, cost, accessibility key in next phase of AI race, says Sundar Pichai
summary: ​Speaking to the media ahead of Google’s annual developer conference Google I/O, Pichai said the industry is entering a phase where efficiency and scale of deployment matter as much as raw model size.

title: Swipe right for AI skills, India Inc to job seekers
summary: India's tech job market saw a significant surge in AI skill demand in January, with nearly a seventh of postings requiring these abilities.
